name.description: Name of the action (must be unique within the project)

description.description: Description of what this action represents

event.description: Event name (e.g., '$pageview', '$autocapture', or custom event name)

steps.description: Action steps - each step defines a trigger condition. Multiple steps are OR-ed together.

post_to_slack.description: Whether to post to Slack when this action is triggered

actionId.description: The ID of the action to delete

limit.description: Maximum number of actions to return

offset.description: Number of actions to skip for pagination

actionId.description: The ID of the action to retrieve
The ID of the action to update

event.description: Event name (e.g., '$pageview', '$autocapture', or custom event name)

pinned_at.description: Pin timestamp (set to pin, null to unpin)

dashboardId.description: The ID of the dashboard to reorder tiles on

tileOrder.description: Array of tile IDs in the desired order from top to bottom

message.description: Optional message to include in the demo data

query.description: Search query to find entities by name or description

entities.description: Entity types to search. If not specified, searches all types. Available: insight, dashboard, experiment, feature_flag, notebook, action, cohort, event_definition, survey

issueId.description: The ID of the error tracking issue to update

status.description: The new status for the issue: active, resolved, archived, suppressed, or pending_release

description.description: Description explaining when the event is triggered

tags.description: Tags to organize events by product area (e.g. "checkout", "onboarding") or user journey stage (e.g. "acquisition", "activation", "monetization", "retention")

verified.description: Mark as verified to indicate the event is properly instrumented and tracking correctly

hidden.description: Mark event as no longer used/captured. Hides it from UI while preserving historical data

eventName.description: The name of the event to update (e.g. "$pageview", "user_signed_up")

description.description: Description explaining when the event is triggered

tags.description: Tags to organize events by product area (e.g. "checkout", "onboarding") or user journey stage (e.g. "acquisition", "activation", "monetization", "retention")

verified.description: Mark as verified to indicate the event is properly instrumented and tracking correctly

hidden.description: Mark event as no longer used/captured. Hides it from UI while preserving historical data

data.description: The event definition data to update

query.description: The final SQL query to be executed.

truncate.description: Whether to truncate large blob/JSON values in results. Defaults to true. Set to false when you need full untruncated results (e.g., for dumping to a file).

name.description: Experiment name - should clearly describe what is being tested

description.description: Detailed description of the experiment hypothesis, what changes are being tested, and expected outcomes

feature_flag_key.description: Feature flag key (letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores only). IMPORTANT: First search for existing feature flags that might be suitable using the feature-flags-get-all tool, then suggest reusing existing ones or creating a new key based on the experiment name

primary_metrics.description: Primary metrics to measure experiment success. IMPORTANT: Each metric needs event_name to track data. For funnels, provide funnel_steps array with event names for each step. Ask user what events they track, or use project-property-definitions to find available events.

metric_type.description: Metric type: 'mean' for average values (revenue, time spent), 'funnel' for conversion flows, 'ratio' for comparing two metrics

event_name.description: REQUIRED for metrics to work: PostHog event name (e.g., '$pageview', 'add_to_cart', 'purchase'). For funnels, this is the first step. Use '$pageview' if unsure. Search project-property-definitions tool for available events.

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names for each funnel step (e.g., ['product_view', 'add_to_cart', 'checkout', 'purchase'])

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

description.description: What this metric measures and why it's important for the experiment

secondary_metrics.description: Secondary metrics to monitor for potential side effects or additional insights. Each metric needs event_name.

metric_type.description: Metric type: 'mean' for average values, 'funnel' for conversion flows, 'ratio' for comparing two metrics

event_name.description: REQUIRED: PostHog event name. Use '$pageview' if unsure.

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names for each funnel step

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

variants.description: Experiment variants. If not specified, defaults to 50/50 control/test split. Ask user how many variants they need and what each tests

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users to show this variant

minimum_detectable_effect.description: Minimum detectable effect in percentage. Lower values require more users but detect smaller changes. Suggest 20-30% for most experiments

filter_test_accounts.description: Whether to filter out internal test accounts

target_properties.description: Properties to target specific user segments (e.g., country, subscription type)

draft.description: Create as draft (true) or launch immediately (false). Recommend draft for review first

holdout_id.description: Holdout group ID if this experiment should exclude users from other experiments

experimentId.description: The ID of the experiment to delete
The ID of the experiment to retrieve
The ID of the experiment to get comprehensive results for

refresh.description: Force refresh of results instead of using cached values

metric_type.description: Metric type: 'mean' for average values, 'funnel' for conversion flows, 'ratio' for comparing two metrics

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names for each funnel step

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

minimum_detectable_effect.description: Update minimum detectable effect in percentage

launch.description: Launch experiment (set start_date) or keep as draft

restart.description: Restart concluded experiment as draft (clears start_date, end_date, and conclusion)

experimentId.description: The ID of the experiment to update

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names for each funnel step

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

launch.description: Launch experiment (set start_date) or keep as draft

restart.description: Restart concluded experiment as draft (clears start_date, end_date, and conclusion)

data.description: The experiment data to update using user-friendly format

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

source.description: For new insights, use the query from your successful query-run tool call. For updates, the existing query can optionally be reused.

question.description: Your natural language query describing the SQL insight (max 1000 characters).

source.description: For new insights, use the query from your successful query-run tool call. For updates, the existing query can optionally be reused

key.description: The attribute key to get values for

attributeType.description: Type of attribute: "log" for log attributes, "resource" for resource attributes (default: log)
Type of attributes to list: "log" for log attributes, "resource" for resource attributes (default: log)

dateFrom.description: Start of date range (ISO 8601 format, e.g., "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z")

dateTo.description: End of date range (ISO 8601 format, e.g., "2024-01-02T00:00:00Z")

severityLevels.description: Filter by severity levels (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)

searchTerm.description: Free text search term to filter logs

after.description: Cursor for pagination (from previous response nextCursor)

q.description: Search query to filter event names. Only use if there are lots of events.

eventName.description: Event name to filter properties by, required for event type

event_name.description: The name of the event that you want to retrieve properties for.

entity.description: The type of the entity that you want to retrieve properties for.

action_id.description: The ID of the action that you want to retrieve properties for.

entity.description: The type of the entity that you want to retrieve properties for.

property_name.description: Verified property name of an entity.
Verified property name of an event.
Verified property name of an action.

query.description: The data schema query to execute.

ReadDataWarehouseSchemaSchema.description: No input required. Returns core data warehouse schemas.

display.description: Display format: 'number' shows numeric scale, 'emoji' shows emoji scale

scale.description: Rating scale can be one of 3, 5, or 7

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for the lowest rating (e.g., 'Very Poor')

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

anyOf.description: For rating questions: use sentiment keys based on scale thirds - negative (lower third), neutral (middle third), positive (upper third)

display.description: NPS questions always use numeric scale

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for 0 rating (typically 'Not at all likely')

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

anyOf.description: For NPS rating questions: use sentiment keys based on score ranges - detractors (0-6), passives (7-8), promoters (9-10)

choices.description: Array of choice options. Choice indices (0, 1, 2, etc.) are used for branching logic

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

anyOf.description: For single choice questions: use choice indices as string keys ("0", "1", "2", etc.)

choices.description: Array of choice options. Multiple selections allowed. No branching logic supported.

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

start_date.description: Setting this will launch the survey immediately. Don't add a start_date unless explicitly requested to do so.

responses_limit.description: The maximum number of responses before automatically stopping the survey.

iteration_count.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the number of times the survey should be shown to the user. Use 1 for 'once every X days', higher numbers for multiple repetitions. Works together with iteration_frequency_days to determine the overall survey schedule.

iteration_frequency_days.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the interval in days between each survey instance shown to the user, used alongside iteration_count for precise scheduling.

enable_partial_responses.description: When at least one question is answered, the response is stored (true). The response is stored when all questions are answered (false).

linked_flag_id.description: The feature flag linked to this survey

targeting_flag_filters.description: Target specific users based on their properties. Example: {groups: [{properties: [{key: 'email', value: ['@company.com'], operator: 'icontains'}], rollout_percentage: 100}]}

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

date_from.description: Optional ISO timestamp for start date (e.g. 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z)

date_to.description: Optional ISO timestamp for end date (e.g. 2024-01-31T23:59:59Z)

date_from.description: Optional ISO timestamp for start date (e.g. 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z)

date_to.description: Optional ISO timestamp for end date (e.g. 2024-01-31T23:59:59Z)

display.description: Display format: 'number' shows numeric scale, 'emoji' shows emoji scale

scale.description: Rating scale can be one of 3, 5, or 7

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for the lowest rating (e.g., 'Very Poor')

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

anyOf.description: For rating questions: use sentiment keys based on scale thirds - negative (lower third), neutral (middle third), positive (upper third)

display.description: NPS questions always use numeric scale

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for 0 rating (typically 'Not at all likely')

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

anyOf.description: For NPS rating questions: use sentiment keys based on score ranges - detractors (0-6), passives (7-8), promoters (9-10)

choices.description: Array of choice options. Choice indices (0, 1, 2, etc.) are used for branching logic

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

anyOf.description: For single choice questions: use choice indices as string keys ("0", "1", "2", etc.)

choices.description: Array of choice options. Multiple selections allowed. No branching logic supported.

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

seenSurveyWaitPeriodInDays.description: Don't show this survey to users who saw any survey in the last x days.

urlMatchType.description: URL/device matching types: 'regex' (matches regex pattern), 'not_regex' (does not match regex pattern), 'exact' (exact string match), 'is_not' (not exact match), 'icontains' (case-insensitive contains), 'not_icontains' (case-insensitive does not contain)

repeatedActivation.description: Whether to show the survey every time one of the events is triggered (true), or just once (false)

values.description: Array of event names that trigger the survey

deviceTypesMatchType.description: URL/device matching types: 'regex' (matches regex pattern), 'not_regex' (does not match regex pattern), 'exact' (exact string match), 'is_not' (not exact match), 'icontains' (case-insensitive contains), 'not_icontains' (case-insensitive does not contain)

linkedFlagVariant.description: The variant of the feature flag linked to this survey

schedule.description: Survey scheduling behavior: 'once' = show once per user (default), 'recurring' = repeat based on iteration_count and iteration_frequency_days settings, 'always' = show every time conditions are met (mainly for widget surveys)

start_date.description: When the survey should start being shown to users. Setting this will launch the survey

end_date.description: When the survey stopped being shown to users. Setting this will complete the survey.

responses_limit.description: The maximum number of responses before automatically stopping the survey.

iteration_count.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the number of times the survey should be shown to the user. Use 1 for 'once every X days', higher numbers for multiple repetitions. Works together with iteration_frequency_days to determine the overall survey schedule.

iteration_frequency_days.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the interval in days between each survey instance shown to the user, used alongside iteration_count for precise scheduling.

enable_partial_responses.description: When at least one question is answered, the response is stored (true). The response is stored when all questions are answered (false).

linked_flag_id.description: The feature flag to link to this survey

targeting_flag_id.description: An existing targeting flag to use for this survey

targeting_flag_filters.description: Target specific users based on their properties. Example: {groups: [{properties: [{key: 'email', value: ['@company.com'], operator: 'icontains'}], rollout_percentage: 50}]}

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

remove_targeting_flag.description: Set to true to completely remove all targeting filters from the survey, making it visible to all users (subject to other display conditions like URL matching).
